source: Codified from cluster walkthrough — Andrew's vetted feedback on 46 proposed clusters. Each gold belief lists evidence_signals (existing obs titles in DB) so PR3.6 can verify the extractor reproduces these claims.
design_rules: See memory/memory-graph-design.md → 'Belief former design rules'. Claims must be rule-like, owner-scoped > property-scoped, garbage disposals = plumbing, single-obs beliefs only with stated rule language.

beliefs.claim: Yonic Herrera handles all plumbing issues — drains, garbage disposals, water heaters, faucets, complex repairs.
evidence_signals: 223-7 Union Place hot water → Yonic
227-14 Union Place faucets → Yonic
Yonic plumbing → preferred (stated rule: 'Yonic is a plumber, more experienced')
1517 W 12th Pl hot water → Yonic
829 Ocean Park Blvd → Yonic (garbage disposal)
1951 22nd St → Yonic (clogged bathtub drain)
Pickford disposal → Yonic
beliefs.notes: Stated explicitly by Jose. Garbage disposals categorized as plumbing (NOT appliance).

beliefs.claim: Abraham Monroy handles general handyman issues AND simple plumbing (toilet basics, slow drains, doorknobs, window screens, curtain rods, blinds).
evidence_signals: 22nd Street unit 4 plumbing → Abraham (toilet refill + slow shower drain)
180-10 11th St toilet handle → Abraham
Harvard disposal → Abraham
11th St blinds → Abraham
829 Ocean Park unit 6 curtain rod → Abraham
908 15th Street unit 4 drain cover → Abraham
1951 22nd St → Abraham (window screens)
Van Buren 2919 1/2 doorknob + drains → Abraham (Jose: 'simple enough for him')
Walnut Lane unit 5 → Abraham (Jose: 'simple enough')
11963 Walnut → Abraham (screen door)
beliefs.notes: Counter-signal to Yonic for SIMPLE plumbing — explicit 'simple enough for Abraham' phrasing from Jose. Belief former should compare issue complexity to choose Abraham vs Yonic.

beliefs.claim: Kori Anderson handles all maintenance (handyman + simple plumbing + electrical) at Harrison Properties.
evidence_signals: Ozone closet door → Kori (17 Ozone Ave, Harrison Properties 2 LLC)
Rose Ave smoke alarm → Kori (50 Rose Ave, Harrison)
50 Rose oven door → Kori
294 Ozone power outage → Kori (15 Ozone Ave, Harrison)
50 Rose dishwasher (Kori diagnosed before Waadt)
15 Ozone unit 1 drain → Kori (slow shower drain — simple plumbing)
19 1/2 Ozone flood → Kori
50 Rose Ave drain → Kori
beliefs.notes: Owner-scoped belief (Harrison Properties LLC AND Harrison Properties 2 LLC both qualify). Generalizes to all properties owned by Harrison entities. Counter to the simple/complex plumbing rule: at Harrison, Kori does simple plumbing as part of handyman scope (instead of Abraham).

beliefs.claim: Cross Appliance Inc handles all appliance issues — fridges, dishwashers, ice makers, dryers, ranges.
evidence_signals: 15th Street fridge leak → Cross Appliance
50 Rose dishwasher → Waad/Cross flow (after Waadt declined repairs)
50 Rose dishwasher → replace approved (Waadt, but Cross is the standing appliance vendor)
Budlong ice maker → Cross Appliance
223 Union Place → Cross Appliance (leaking fridge)
beliefs.notes: Vendor-wide capability. Appliance domain excludes garbage disposals (those are plumbing → Yonic).

beliefs.claim: Osalpa Electric handles all electrical issues across the portfolio.
evidence_signals: 22nd Street outlet → Jose called Osalpa directly (phone troubleshoot)
Ocean Park electrical → Osalpa (kitchen power loss + microwave tripping)
1520 Ocean Park C lights → Osalpa (flickering)
1950 Cloverfield breaker → Osalpa would-have-been-dispatched (tenant resolved)
beliefs.notes: Default electrical vendor. Counter case: Osalpa failed to diagnose flickering at 1520 OP unit C (S32 follow-up) — agent should escalate to a different electrician on recurrence.

beliefs.claim: Primex Termite Co. handles all pest issues at the portfolio's properties.
evidence_signals: Pickford pest spray → Primex (chose over Dever)
Primrose rats → Primex (recurring)
6337 Primrose Ave → Primex dispatched (recurring rat infestation)
beliefs.notes: Default pest vendor. Jose has shown preference for Primex even over closer competitors (Dever).

beliefs.claim: Jimenez Services is the primary vendor for gates and gate-related issues.
evidence_signals: Jimenez Services → primary gates vendor (stated rule from Jose)
beliefs.notes: Single-obs belief because Jose explicitly stated it as a rule. May also cover garage doors (Jose corrected agent from 'garage door' to 'gates' — implying overlap).

beliefs.claim: LA's Best Rain Gutters Solutions handles gutters at 31135 Palos Verdes Dr E.
evidence_signals: Palos Verdes gutters → LA's Best Gutters (Jose picked outside JL/Luis suggestions)
beliefs.notes: Property-scoped. May generalize to all gutter work eventually, but only one obs so keep scoped.

beliefs.claim: L.A.HYDRO-JET & ROOTER SERVICE, INC. handles drain work at 31135 Palos Verdes Dr E.
evidence_signals: Palos Verdes drain → LA HydroJet (Jose-stated routing)
beliefs.notes: Drain specialist for Palos Verdes specifically. Single obs but stated routing. May generalize if more obs appear.

beliefs.claim: Guox Maintenance & Remodel handles exterior lighting and multi-handyman fixes at 6337 Primrose Ave (lights, fan light, blinds, screen door handle).
evidence_signals: Primrose exterior light → Guox Maintenance
Primrose unit 9 → Guox dispatched (fan light + blinds + screen door handle)
6337 Primrose plumbing → Guox (recent S36 bonus session)
beliefs.notes: Property-scoped. Guox is the preferred handyman at Primrose (not the same as Abraham elsewhere).

beliefs.claim: Tre Elevators is the contracted elevator service vendor for 6337 Primrose Ave. Generalizes: for any property with an elevator, contact the property's contracted elevator company — not Guox or other handymen.
evidence_signals: Primrose elevator → Tre Elevators (Jose: 'Primerose has a contract with Tre elevators')
Negative: Don't send Guox for elevator issues at 6337 Primrose (Jose declined)
beliefs.notes: Stated explicitly by Jose with 'remember this' framing. Highest confidence. Generalization to all elevator-equipped properties is implied: 'The properties that have elevators have contracts with specific companies.'

beliefs.claim: Solomon Grauzinis Trust requires owner approval before vendor dispatch for ALL issues at their properties.
evidence_signals: 828 11th St oven door → owner approval (Jose: 'we need approval for most things')
Solomon Grauzinis Trust → approval required (Jose verbatim confirmation)
828 11th St dryer → owner approval required
beliefs.notes: STATED RULE. Counter case: S5 CO alarm at 828 11th was self-handled without approval — suggests safety/urgent issues bypass. Refinement may be: 'approval required for all NON-emergency issues.' Owner-scope covers both Solomon Grauzinis Trust + Grauzinis ME Trust (co-owners of 828 11th St).

beliefs.claim: Twelfth Place LLC properties require owner discussion before vendor dispatch for damage-related issues (e.g., water leaks).
evidence_signals: W 12th Place toilet leak → owner discussion (Jose deferred dispatch)
beliefs.notes: Issue-type scoped within owner-scope. Counter case: 1517 W 12th hot water (S23) did NOT require owner approval. So approval is for damage-class issues, not routine plumbing.

beliefs.claim: Anthony Lee (213 S. Arnaz Dr owner) requires owner approval before vendor selection; he picks the vendor himself.
evidence_signals: 213 S Arnaz → owner decides vendor (Jose deferred to owner)
beliefs.notes: Owner-scoped. Stronger than typical approval — Anthony picks the vendor, not just approves.

beliefs.claim: Harrison Properties 2 LLC requires owner approval for cost-significant repairs (e.g., skylight replacements ~$850).
evidence_signals: 19 Ozone skylight → owner approval (Precision Roofing $850 quote sent to owner)
beliefs.notes: Owner-scoped cost-significant gate. Different from Harrison Properties LLC (which doesn't seem to have this gate). Refinement may be needed across more obs.

beliefs.claim: Laundry machine issues at 144 S. Westmoreland Ave route to the laundry-machine company directly; LAPM does not own the machines.
evidence_signals: 144 S. Westmoreland laundry → outside vendor (Jose stated 'we don't own the machine')
beliefs.notes: Property-specific ownership-of-equipment exception. Single obs but stated rule — high confidence.

beliefs.claim: Unit 14 tenant at 221-229 Union Pl serves as the onsite manager; route property-level questions through them, not the owner.
evidence_signals: 227 Union Place → onsite manager (Jose: 'he is the onsite manager. I'll talk to him on Monday')
beliefs.notes: Tenant-anchored fact (schema gap — no tenant entity kind, anchored to property for now). High confidence — stated by Jose.

beliefs.claim: For weekend electrical issues, ask tenants to troubleshoot (check breakers / reset) before scheduling an electrician for Monday.
evidence_signals: 829 Ocean Park unit 6 → tenant troubleshoot first (Jose's tiered weekend response)
beliefs.notes: Vendor-less procedural rule. Single obs but rule-like phrasing from Jose. Could escalate confidence with more weekend examples.

beliefs.claim: Jose personally handles property-management situations (security, homeless encampments, tenant escalations, owner-relations) rather than dispatching a vendor.
evidence_signals: 50 Rose Ave (Rose Avenue Unit 8) → Jose self-handled homeless encampment
Primrose call box → Jose self-handled
Westmoreland noise complaint → Jose handled
Inglewood disposal recurring → tenant talk (Jose suspected tenant misuse)
beliefs.notes: Property-management domain belief — distinct from maintenance self-handle. Jose IS the PM, so these are out-of-scope for vendor dispatch.

beliefs.claim: Jose frequently self-handles minor maintenance repairs himself (small fixes, quick stops, water heater checks).
evidence_signals: Glencoe fire alarm → JL
Primrose garage → Jose self-handled
17th Street hot water → Jose self-handled
11th St carbon monoxide → Jose self-handled
Glencoe toilet seat → Jose self-handled
918 12th Street heatlamp → Jose self-handled
5455 Inglewood + 829 OP disposals → Jose self-handled (S28 cluster)
beliefs.notes: Pattern A self-handle for maintenance domain. Counter case to vendor dispatch — Jose preempts vendor when issue is small or his calendar permits. Belief former should learn it's an OPTION, not always the default.

beliefs.claim: Jose sometimes coordinates with vendors or tenants directly by phone instead of full dispatch.
evidence_signals: 22nd Street outlet → Jose called Osalpa directly
1520 Ocean Park flickering recur → Jose calls tenant
beliefs.notes: Procedural pattern — agent should sometimes propose phone coordination instead of full dispatch when issue is diagnostic-ambiguous or recurring.

do_not_emit.reason: Vendor is removed from system. No future dispatch decisions reference Darwin. Just mark inactive.

do_not_emit.category: New vendor added (admin event, not belief)
do_not_emit.reason: Tool call to add to vendor table. No teachable rule.

do_not_emit.category: Status confirmation (should not have been an obs at all)
do_not_emit.reason: Confirmation of resolution. Reinforces JL self-handle obs but doesn't add belief content.

do_not_emit.signal: Primrose elevator → elevator company (handled by Jose contact) — merged with Tre Elevators belief
do_not_emit.reason: Duplicate of the Tre Elevators stated-rule belief; no separate belief needed.
One-off; reinforces 'Jose handles property-mgmt' belief but doesn't create its own.
